content: (Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): In the current scene as seen by MJR, the view opens into a dimly lit stone chamber with thick, arched walls of rough-hewn masonry, evoking an ancient, foreboding atmosphere. Heavy wooden doors and shelves line the walls, their surfaces etched with age and shadow. A ornate candelabrum flickers with several tall candles, casting warm, wavering light across a sturdy wooden desk cluttered with parchments and inkwells.)

Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

Phinis Gestor: That Falmer manuscript you've been hoarding, Urag—why not let me take a look? Unless you'd rather keep jerking off to those filthy Daedric sketches in the back shelves again. Urag gro-Shub: Keep your necromancer hands off my collection, Breton. Daedric sketches Phinis Gestor: studied
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Phinis Gestor: Gods, this place reeks of moldy parchment and orc musk. How you tolerate this dungeon library is beyond me. Urag gro-Shub: Better than the stench of rotting corpses you conjurers drag in here. At least my robes don’t have suspicious… like yours. Phinis Gestor: Oh please, as if you aren’t sneaking off to the Midden after hours to pay trolls for their . I’ve seen your scrapbook of sketches—Dwemer constructs and flesh atronachs in positions. Urag gro-Shub: Says the man whose last apprentice was found naked in Fellglow Keep’s cellar, strapped to a Daedric summoning circle. Phinis Gestor: , Urag. And she Urag gro-Shub: Hmph. tragic accident

(Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): MJR stands in the dimly lit nave of a grand stone hall, its high vaulted ceiling lost in shadows above. To the left, a tall arched window pierces the thick masonry wall, its leaded panes filled with darkness outside, allowing faint slivers of moonlight to filter through. Sturdy stone pillars rise along the sides, supporting the weight of the ancient structure, while hanging lanterns sway gently, their flames flickering and casting warm pools of light across the cold floor.)

Lydia: By the tight ass of Diabella, this cursed pile of rocks smells worse than a skeever's cunt.
